verdict_reason: Both disagreements are doctrinally substantive. (1) āpūryamāṇam acala-pratiṣṭham — the explanation correctly identifies the structural agreement (the ocean is FULL and STEADY; the waters ENTER it; the level does not rise) and the divergence (Shankara: boundary-preservation under input; Ramanuja: engine-of-pursuit does not engage; Prabhupada: always still; Mukundananda: undisturbed). The note that the structural reading must distinguish receiving from refusing is critical to avoid the impassivity misread — the explanation surfaces this explicitly. (2) kāma-kāmī — the explanation correctly identifies the reduplicative force ('desire-desirer') and notes that Prabhupada and Mukundananda flatten to 'striving to satisfy desires', losing the reduplicative force. The traditional readings preserve it as the active engine of pursuit. Both explanations would survive Sanskrit-grammarian + comparative-philosophy review. Pass.
